CheckTitle: Databricks workspace has public network access disabled
Description: **Azure Databricks** workspaces are evaluated for **public network access**. Disabling public access ensures the workspace is only reachable through private connectivity, reducing exposure to internet-based attacks.
Risk: Public network access exposes the workspace control plane and data plane to the internet. Attackers can attempt **credential stuffing**, exploit vulnerabilities, or exfiltrate data through the public endpoint.

Code.Other:
1. Sign in to Azure portal
2. Go to Azure Databricks
3. Select the workspace
4. Go to Networking
5. Set Public network access to Disabled
6. Configure private endpoints
7. Click Save
Code.Terraform:
```hcl
resource "azurerm_databricks_workspace" "<example_resource_name>" {
  name                          = "<example_resource_name>"
  location                      = "<example_resource_id>"
  resource_group_name           = "<example_resource_id>"
  sku                           = "premium"
  public_network_access_enabled = false  # Critical: disables public network access
}
```

Recommendation.Text: Disable **public network access** on Databricks workspaces and configure **private endpoints** for secure connectivity. Use **Azure Private Link** for the control plane and data plane. Requires Premium SKU tier. Verify all users and automation can reach the workspace through private connectivity before disabling public access.
